message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My task: add fallback_searchpath param to FileSystemLoader in loaders.py (get_source, list_templates). Need to know your feature/files so we can avoid collisions, especially if you also touch loaders.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My task: add optional path_transform parameter to loader classes in src/jinja2/loaders.py (FileSystemLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader). Need to own this file. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: We both must touch FileSystemLoader in loaders.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposing SEQUENTIAL ownership instead: I (agent1) edit loaders.py first, ONLY adding fallback_searchpath to FileSystemLoader.__init__/get_source/list_templates - a small additive change (new optional kwarg + fallback lookup, no changes to existing signatures/logic order). I will NOT touch DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, or ChoiceLoader. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact lines changed when finished. Please hold off editing loaders.py until you get my DONE, then re-read the file fresh and layer path_transform on top (including into FileSystemLoader if needed) around my changes. Agree?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Both features genuinely need loaders.py (mine: path_transform on BaseLoader/FileSystemLoader/PackageLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader; yours: fallback_searchpath on FileSystemLoader). File-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: within FileSystemLoader, you own the fallback/search-loop logic in get_source + list_templates; I'll only add a path_transform param to __init__ (extra kwarg, stored as self.path_transform) and one line at the top of get_source that rewrites  via the transform before your search loop runs - not touching your loop internals. I'll do the same lightweight pattern (constructor kwarg + one-line rewrite at top of get_source/get_loader) for the other loader classes. This should keep our hunks in different lines. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py (FileSystemLoader.__init__ fallback_searchpath kwarg + fallback search-loop logic in get_source and list_templates)
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py (path_transform kwarg in __init__ of all loader classes + one-line rewrite at top of get_source/get_loader; for FileSystemLoader please add your path_transform kwarg to __init__ after mine, and your rewrite line at the very top of get_source before my search loop, without altering my loop code)
note: Agreed. I'll implement now and send DONE with exact method/line summary so you can layer your changes in cleanly afterward.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed - sequential ownership works well. Go ahead and edit loaders.py first for fallback_searchpath (FileSystemLoader only). I will hold off editing until I receive your DONE, then re-read the file fresh and layer path_transform on top across BaseLoader/FileSystemLoader/PackageLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader, keeping my additions additive (new optional kwarg + minimal insertion points) so I do not disturb your fallback_searchpath logic.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished. Changes: (1) added 'from itertools import chain' to imports near top. (2) FileSystemLoader.__init__ signature is now (self, searchpath, encoding='utf-8', followlinks=False, fallback_searchpath=None) - I added fallback_searchpath as the LAST param, so if you add path_transform to this same __init__ please add it after fallback_searchpath to avoid positional-arg confusion, and add the docstring param entry after mine. (3) self.fallback_searchpath is set at the end of __init__ (a list, empty if None). (4) get_source and list_templates now iterate 'chain(self.searchpath, self.fallback_searchpath)' instead of just 'self.searchpath' - if you add a one-line path_transform rewrite at the top of get_source, put it BEFORE the 'pieces = split_template_path(template)' line so it applies regardless of which searchpath list ends up matching. Tests pass (tests/test_loader.py, 40 passed). Go ahead and edit now - file is clear from me.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished path_transform. Added BaseLoader.__init__(path_transform=None) storing self.path_transform, and each subclass (FileSystemLoader, PackageLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) now takes path_transform as its LAST constructor kwarg and calls super().__init__(path_transform) first thing. In each get_source (and PrefixLoader.get_loader / ChoiceLoader.load), I save 'original = template' then rewrite 'template = self.path_transform(template)' if set, using 'original' in the final TemplateNotFound raise so error messages show the untransformed name. For FileSystemLoader.get_source my insertion is right after the docstring/signature and before 'pieces = split_template_path(template)', so it shouldn't touch your fallback_searchpath loop body itself - just be aware my param is appended after yours in __init__, and I also changed the final 'raise TemplateNotFound(template)' at the end of get_source to use 'original' instead - please double check that line doesn't collide with any fallback-related change you made there. All 841 tests pass on my end. Full patch written to patch.txt.
